metaTitle: Image Blur — Blur Photos & Hide Sensitive Info (Free, Private & Offline)
metaDescription: Blur your images instantly in your browser to protect privacy or create artistic effects. 100% private — your photos never leave your device. No upload required.

howto.description: Click the upload area or drag and drop the image (JPG, PNG, WebP) you want to blur. Your file remains securely on your device.
Use the slider to control the blur strength. You can see the changes in real-time on the preview window.
Ensure that sensitive information is sufficiently hidden or the desired artistic effect is achieved before saving.
Click 'Blur & Save' to process. Your modified image will be ready for instant download to your device.

why: 100% private — your sensitive images are processed locally and never uploaded
Real-time preview allows you to see exactly how much blur is applied
Perfect for hiding faces, license plates, or confidential text in screenshots
Create beautiful bokeh or soft-focus effects for your social media photos
Works completely offline after the initial page load
No registration, no watermarks, and no file size limits — 100% free
Supports all modern image formats including JPG, PNG, and WebP

faq.q: Is my sensitive data safe when blurring images here?
faq.a: Yes. This tool uses client-side processing. Unlike other online editors, your images are never sent to a server, providing the highest level of privacy for censoring confidential documents or personal photos.

faq.q: Why would I need to blur an entire image?
faq.a: Common uses include hiding faces for privacy compliance, blurring background distractions in portraits, censoring sensitive details in screenshots, or creating soft textures for design backgrounds.

faq.q: Is the blur effect reversible after saving?
faq.a: No. Once an image is blurred and saved, the original pixel data is permanently altered. We recommend always keeping a copy of your original, unblurred file just in case.

faq.q: Can I blur only a specific part of the image?
faq.a: This current version applies a uniform blur to the entire image. For selective blurring of specific areas (like just a face), you may need a more advanced layered photo editor.

faq.q: Does this tool detect faces automatically?
faq.a: No, this tool does not use AI face detection to ensure maximum speed and offline capability. You have full manual control over the blur intensity via the slider.

faq.q: Will the output format be the same as the original?
faq.a: Yes. The tool typically exports the blurred image in the same format (JPG, PNG, or WebP) as the original file you provided.
